title: Add levels and sword weapons to the 3D fighting game

requirements.text: Implement a multi-level progression system in the 3D fighting game. The game should have at least 3 distinct levels/stages. Each level should increase in difficulty (e.g., more Sukuna enemies, faster/stronger enemies, or more enemies simultaneously). After defeating all enemies in a level, the player advances to the next level with a brief level-transition screen displaying the level number. Completing all levels triggers a final victory screen. The current level number should be displayed in the game HUD alongside the existing health bars.
acceptanceCriteria: The game has at least 3 playable levels with increasing difficulty.
Defeating all enemies in a level advances the player to the next level.
A level-transition screen is shown between levels, displaying the current level number.
Completing all levels shows a final victory screen.
The current level number is shown in the HUD alongside health bars.
Enemy count, speed, or strength scales with each level.

requirements.text: Add a sword weapon for UG in the 3D fighting game. UG should be able to equip and use a sword via a dedicated key (e.g., 'E' on keyboard). When the sword is active, UG's melee attack range and damage are increased compared to the default punch/kick. The sword should be rendered as a 3D mesh (e.g., a long, thin box or cylinder with an emissive blade material) visibly held in UG's hand. A sword slash visual effect (arc or sweep emissive geometry) should appear during the attack animation. The sword attack should also be triggerable via a new on-screen SWORD button in the OnScreenControls overlay, following the same mousedown/mouseup and touchstart/touchend pattern as existing buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: UG can activate a sword attack using a dedicated keyboard key (e.g., 'E').
A 3D sword mesh is rendered in UG's hand when the sword attack is triggered.
The sword attack deals more damage and/or has greater range than default melee attacks.
A visible sword slash arc or sweep emissive effect appears during the attack animation.
A SWORD button is added to the OnScreenControls overlay and triggers the sword attack on tap/click with mousedown/mouseup and touchstart/touchend support.
The sword attack integrates with the existing combat and hit-detection systems, causing Sukuna to lose health on a successful sword hit.

requirements.text: Extend the KeyState and GameState type definitions in frontend/src/types/game.ts to include a 'sword' boolean key for the new sword attack input, a 'currentLevel' number field in game state, and any necessary sword-attack state (e.g., swordActive flag, swordDamageMultiplier) on the Character interface, so the levels and sword systems integrate cleanly with existing TypeScript types.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState or equivalent top-level state includes a 'currentLevel' number field.
Character interface includes a swordActive flag or equivalent sword-attack state.
All existing usages of KeyState and Character remain type-safe after the additions.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Retain all existing keyboard controls, on-screen controls, gun system, health bar UI, and visual effects without regression.
The sword attack must integrate with the existing combatSystem.ts hit-detection logic rather than bypass it.
nonGoals: Adding new enemy character types other than Sukuna.
Backend changes — level and sword state is purely frontend/client-side.
Changing the calendar or sanctuary features.
